other_observers: Tony Leukering had 6 that day. Many other observers.;
duration: Maybe a half hour total over several hours
specific_location: Mudflats of western side of Prewitt Reservoir, mainly in front of the boat ramp
description: See included photos. Mid-sized, compact-bodied, yellow-legged sandpiper. Fairly short, straight dark bill, blank looking buffy face with large appearing dark eye gives bird a dove-like cute look. Crown with dense, dark spots. Upper breast and upper flanks with dark spots against buffy background. Crisp, pale edging on upperpart feathers indicate juvenile age. Underparts mostly unmarked buffy white. Wings fairly short, just reaching tail tip.Behaviors: Foraging on mudflats. Often far from the water's edge, but occasionally within 10 meters of water. Never seen in the shallow water.Call: Not heardPlumage: Juvenile
